1827	// TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorMessageInOutput verifies that when the agent runner
1828	// returns an error, the error message appears in the JSON output Error field.
1829	func TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorMessageInOutput(t *testing.T) {
1830		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1831			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
1832			wtHeadRev:   "aaaa1111", // no commits made
1833		}
1834		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{err: fmt.Errorf("agent crashed with detail"), result: nil}
1835		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1836	
1837		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
1838	
1839		assert.Equal(t, 1, res.ExitCode)
1840		assert.Equal(t, "agent crashed with detail", res.Error)
1841	}
1842	
1843	// TestExecuteBeadHeadRevFailure verifies that when HeadRev fails after the agent
1844	// runs, the outcome is "error" and the reason contains the original error message.
1845	// This covers the path at agent_execute_bead.go lines 282-309.
1846	func TestExecuteBeadHeadRevFailure(t *testing.T) {
1847		t.Run("json output", func(t *testing.T) {
1848			git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1849				mainHeadRev:  "aaaa1111",
1850				wtHeadRevErr: fmt.Errorf("disk read error"),
1851			}
1852			runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
1853			f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1854	
1855			root := f.NewRootCommand()
1856			out, cmdErr := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json")
1857			require.Error(t, cmdErr)
1858			res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
1859	
1860			assert.Equal(t, "error", res.Outcome)
1861			assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, res.Status)
1862			assert.Contains(t, res.Reason, "disk read error")
1863			assert.Equal(t, 1, res.ExitCode)
1864		})
1865	
1866		t.Run("text output", func(t *testing.T) {
1867			git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1868				mainHeadRev:  "aaaa1111",
1869				wtHeadRevErr: fmt.Errorf("disk read error"),
1870			}
1871			runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
1872			f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1873	
1874			root := f.NewRootCommand()
1875			out, cmdErr := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead")
1876			require.Error(t, cmdErr)
1877	
1878			assert.Contains(t, out, "outcome: error")
1879			assert.Contains(t, out, "disk read error")
1880		})
1881	}

1883	// TestExecuteBeadCompoundErrorAgentAndHeadRevFailure verifies that when the
1884	// agent runner returns an error AND HeadRev fails on the worktree, both the
1885	// Error field (agent message) and the Reason field (rev error) are present in
1886	// the JSON output. This covers the path at agent_execute_bead.go that
1887	// previously dropped the agent error message when revErr was non-nil.
1888	func TestExecuteBeadCompoundErrorAgentAndHeadRevFailure(t *testing.T) {
1889		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1890			mainHeadRev:  "aaaa1111",
1891			wtHeadRevErr: fmt.Errorf("worktree HEAD unreadable"),
1892		}
1893		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{err: fmt.Errorf("agent exploded"), result: nil}
1894		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1895	
1896		root := f.NewRootCommand()
1897		out, cmdErr := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json")
1898		require.Error(t, cmdErr)
1899		res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
1900	
1901		assert.Equal(t, 1, res.ExitCode)
1902		assert.Equal(t, "error", res.Outcome)
1903		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, res.Status)
1904		assert.Equal(t, "agent exploded", res.Error,
1905			"agent error message must be preserved even when HeadRev also fails")
1906		assert.Contains(t, res.Reason, "worktree HEAD unreadable",
1907			"Reason must reflect the HeadRev failure")
1908	}

1910	// TestExecuteBeadInvalidBeadID verifies that beadIDs with characters illegal
1911	// in git ref names are rejected with a clear error before any git or agent
1912	// operations are attempted.
1913	func TestExecuteBeadInvalidBeadID(t *testing.T) {
1914		invalidIDs := []string{
1915			"bead with spaces",
1916			"bead~1",
1917			"bead^1",
1918			"bead:name",
1919			"bead[0]",
1920		}
1921		for _, id := range invalidIDs {
1922			t.Run(id, func(t *testing.T) {
1923				git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111"}
1924				runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
1925				f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1926	
1927				root := f.NewRootCommand()
1928				_, cmdErr := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", id)
1929				require.Error(t, cmdErr)
1930				assert.Contains(t, cmdErr.Error(), "invalid bead ID")
1931	
1932				// No git or agent operations should have been attempted.
1933				assert.Empty(t, git.addedWTs, "no worktree should be created for invalid bead ID")
1934			})
1935		}
1936	}

1938	// TestExecuteBeadEvidenceFields verifies that runtime evidence fields are
1939	// populated in the JSON output.
1940	func TestExecuteBeadEvidenceFields(t *testing.T) {
1941		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1942			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
1943			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222",
1944		}
1945		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{
1946			ExitCode: 0,
1947			Harness:  "testharness",
1948			Model:    "test-model",
1949			Tokens:   42,
1950			CostUSD:  0.001,
1951		}}
1952		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1953	
1954		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
1955	
1956		assert.Equal(t, "testharness", res.Harness)
1957		assert.Equal(t, "test-model", res.Model)
1958		assert.Equal(t, 42, res.Tokens)
1959		assert.InDelta(t, 0.001, res.CostUSD, 1e-9)
1960		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.SessionID)
1961		assert.False(t, res.StartedAt.IsZero())
1962		assert.False(t, res.FinishedAt.IsZero())
1963		assert.Equal(t, "aaaa1111", res.BaseRev)
1964		assert.Equal(t, "bbbb2222", res.ResultRev)
1965	}

1967	// TestExecuteBeadModelFlagPassthrough locks in the resolution contract for
1968	// execute-bead's model option: the value supplied via ExecuteBeadOptions.Model
1969	// is passed verbatim to the runner, and an empty value is not silently replaced
1970	// by any hardcoded or catalog-derived default. This regression test guards
1971	// against routing layers injecting a model (e.g. a stale vendor/model like
1972	// "z-ai/glm-5.1") when the caller did not request one — the case the agent
1973	// harness resolves from ~/.config/agent/config.yaml must be preserved by
1974	// ExecuteBead handing the runner an empty Model so the harness's own
1975	// resolution chain runs.
1976	func TestExecuteBeadModelFlagPassthrough(t *testing.T) {
1977		t.Run("empty model stays empty through ExecuteBead", func(t *testing.T) {
1978			git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1979				mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
1980				wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222",
1981			}
1982			runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
1983			f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1984	
1985			// No --model flag supplied to execute-bead.
1986			runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
1987	
1988			assert.Equal(t, "", runner.last.Model,
1989				"runner must receive an empty Model when no --model flag is provided; "+
1990					"any non-empty value here indicates a routing layer injected a default, "+
1991					"which would override the harness's own config-driven resolution")
1992		})
1993	
1994		t.Run("explicit model is forwarded verbatim", func(t *testing.T) {
1995			git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1996				mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
1997				wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222",
1998			}
1999			runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}

1700		// trip a synthetic failure so runScriptFn returns (result, execErr).
1701		// This mimics the original fakeAgentRunner{err: ..., result: nil} case
1702		// — runner errored after commits were already made. The merge would
1703		// have fast-forwarded cleanly (no sibling advance), so the test proves
1704		// execute-bead preserves based on the error signal alone, not on any
1705		// merge-side failure.
1706		directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
1707		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte(
1708			"create-file crashfile.txt pre-crash-content\n"+
1709				"commit feat: partial work before crash\n"+
1710				"fail-during 2\n",
1711		), 0o644))
1712	
1713		// Real runner + real git ops: no overrides that fake the thing under test.
1714		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
1715		f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
1716	
1717		root := f.NewRootCommand()
1718		out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json",
1719			"--from", baseSHA,
1720			"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
1721		require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
1722		res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
1723	
1724		assert.Equal(t, 1, res.ExitCode)
1725		assert.Equal(t, "preserved", res.Outcome)
1726		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, res.Status)
1727		assert.Equal(t, baseSHA, res.BaseRev)
1728		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.ResultRev)
1729		assert.NotEqual(t, baseSHA, res.ResultRev, "worker must have produced a commit before the failure")
1730		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.PreserveRef)
1731	
1732		// Hidden ref must resolve to the worker's commit, proving it was
1733		// preserved rather than discarded.
1734		preservedSHA := runGit("rev-parse", res.PreserveRef)
1735		assert.Equal(t, res.ResultRev, preservedSHA, "preserve ref must resolve to the worker commit")
1736	
1737		// Target branch must NOT advance — an agent error, even with commits
1738		// and a clean would-have-succeeded merge, must preserve before land.
1739		// (Pre-refactor equivalent: assert.Equal(t, 0, git.mergeCalls).)
1740		assert.Equal(t, baseSHA, runGit("rev-parse", "refs/heads/main"),
1741			"main must be unchanged when agent errors with commits")
1742		mergeCommits := runGit("log", "--merges", "--format=%H", "refs/heads/main")
1743		assert.Empty(t, mergeCommits, "agent-error path must not produce a merge commit on main")
1744	}

1746	// TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorWithCommitsPreserves verifies that when the agent
1747	// runner returns an error and commits exist, exitCode=1 and outcome="preserved"
1748	// with a non-empty, correctly-formatted preserve ref. Exercises real git +
1749	// RealLandingGitOps via the script harness — no fakes for the components under
1750	// test.
1751	func TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorWithCommitsPreserves(t *testing.T) {
1752		workDir := t.TempDir()
1753	
1754		scrubEnv := func() []string {
1755			parent := os.Environ()
1756			env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
1757			for _, kv := range parent {
1758				if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
1759					continue
1760				}
1761				env = append(env, kv)
1762			}
1763			return env
1764		}
1765		runGit := func(args ...string) string {
1766			t.Helper()
1767			cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
1768			cmd.Dir = workDir
1769			cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
1770			out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
1771			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
1772			return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
1773		}
1774	
1775		runGit("init", "-b", "main")
1776		runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
1777		runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
1778		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
1779		runGit("add", ".")
1780		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
1781	
1782		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
1783			ID:        "my-bead",
1784			Title:     "Test execute-bead agent error with commits preserves",
1785			Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
1786			IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
1787		})
1788		runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
1789		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
1790		baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
1791	
1792		// Script harness: create a real commit in the worker worktree, then
1793		// trip a synthetic failure so runScriptFn returns (result, execErr).
1794		// Mirrors the original fakeAgentRunner{err: ..., result: nil} with
1795		// commits present.
1796		directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
1797		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte(
1798			"create-file crashfile.txt pre-crash-content\n"+
1799				"commit feat: partial work before crash\n"+
1800				"fail-during 2\n",
1801		), 0o644))
1802	
1803		// Real runner + real git ops: no overrides that fake the thing under test.
1804		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
1805		f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
1806	
1807		root := f.NewRootCommand()
1808		out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json",
1809			"--from", baseSHA,
1810			"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
1811		require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
1812		res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
1813	
1814		assert.Equal(t, 1, res.ExitCode)
1815		assert.Equal(t, "preserved", res.Outcome)
1816		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, res.Status)
1817		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.ResultRev)
1818		assert.NotEqual(t, baseSHA, res.ResultRev, "worker must have produced a commit before the failure")
1819		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.PreserveRef)
1820		assertPreserveRef(t, res.PreserveRef, "my-bead", baseSHA)
1821	
1822		// Preserve ref must resolve to the worker's commit.
1823		preservedSHA := runGit("rev-parse", res.PreserveRef)
1824		assert.Equal(t, res.ResultRev, preservedSHA, "preserve ref must resolve to the worker commit")
1825	}

718			}
719			agentErrMsg = agentErr.Error()

722		// Capture routing evidence from the agent result. These fields are
723		// populated by RunAgent (embedded harness) and RunScript (script harness).
724		routeReason := ""

732		resultRev, revErr := gitOps.HeadRev(wtPath)

734			res := &ExecuteBeadResult{
735				BeadID:       beadID,
736				AttemptID:    attemptID,
737				WorkerID:     opts.WorkerID,
738				BaseRev:      baseRev,
739				ResultRev:    baseRev, // no commits readable; treat as no output
740				Harness:      resultHarness,
741				Provider:     resultProvider,
742				Model:        resultModel,
743				SessionID:    sessionID,
744				DurationMS:   int(finishedAt.Sub(startedAt).Milliseconds()),
745				Tokens:       tokens,
746				CostUSD:      costUSD,
747				ExitCode:     1,
748				Error:        agentErrMsg,
749				Reason:       revErr.Error(), // HeadRev failure; orchestrator prefers this over Error for Reason
750				ExecutionDir: artifacts.DirRel,
751				PromptFile:   artifacts.PromptRel,
752				ManifestFile: artifacts.ManifestRel,
753				ResultFile:   artifacts.ResultRel,
754				StartedAt:    startedAt,
755				FinishedAt:   finishedAt,
756				Outcome:      ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed,
757			}
758			res.FailureMode = ClassifyFailureMode(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Error)
759			populateWorkerStatus(res)
760			_ = writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ResultAbs, res)
761			return res, fmt.Errorf("failed to read worktree HEAD: %w", revErr)
762		}
763	
764		// Write usage.json when the harness reports token usage or cost.
765		// Done before SynthesizeCommit so usage data is available in the
766		// preliminary result written for commit-message sourcing.
767		var usageFileRel string
768		if tokens > 0 || costUSD > 0 {
769			usage := executeBeadUsage{
770				AttemptID:    attemptID,
771				Harness:      resultHarness,
772				Provider:     resultProvider,
773				Model:        resultModel,
774				Tokens:       tokens,
775				InputTokens:  inputTokens,
776				OutputTokens: outputTokens,
777				CostUSD:      costUSD,
778			}
779			if writeErr := writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.UsageAbs, usage); writeErr == nil {
780				usageFileRel = artifacts.UsageRel
781			}
782		}
783	
784		// Synthesize a commit when the agent left tracked edits without committing.
785		// Only do so for real changes — harness noise paths are excluded. If nothing
786		// real was staged (committed is false), leave resultRev == baseRev so the
787		// outcome is classified as task_no_changes.
788		if resultRev == baseRev {
789			if isDirty, _ := gitOps.IsDirty(wtPath); isDirty {
790				// Build a preliminary result and write it to result.json before
791				// calling SynthesizeCommit. The commit message is then sourced from
792				// the tracked artifact file, satisfying the provenance contract:
793				// "commit-message metadata must be projected from tracked artifact
794				// files, never from ad hoc runtime state" (FEAT-006).
795				// The final result.json is re-written after the commit with the
796				// correct ResultRev, Outcome, and Status.
797				prelimOutcome := ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded
798				if exitCode != 0 {
799					prelimOutcome = ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed
800				}
801				prelimRes := &ExecuteBeadResult{
802					BeadID:       beadID,
803					AttemptID:    attemptID,
804					WorkerID:     opts.WorkerID,
805					BaseRev:      baseRev,
806					ResultRev:    "", // unknown until commit is made
807					Harness:      resultHarness,
808					Provider:     resultProvider,
809					Model:        resultModel,
810					SessionID:    sessionID,
811					DurationMS:   int(finishedAt.Sub(startedAt).Milliseconds()),
812					Tokens:       tokens,
813					CostUSD:      costUSD,
814					ExitCode:     exitCode,
815					Error:        agentErrMsg,
816					ExecutionDir: artifacts.DirRel,
817					PromptFile:   artifacts.PromptRel,
818					ManifestFile: artifacts.ManifestRel,
819					ResultFile:   artifacts.ResultRel,
820					UsageFile:    usageFileRel,
821					StartedAt:    startedAt,
822					FinishedAt:   finishedAt,
823					Outcome:      prelimOutcome,
824				}
825				populateWorkerStatus(prelimRes)
826				_ = writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ResultAbs, prelimRes)
827	
828				// Render the commit message from the tracked artifact file.
829				commitMsg, msgErr := BuildCommitMessageFromResultFile(artifacts.ResultAbs)
830				if msgErr != nil {
831					commitMsg = "chore: execute-bead iteration " + beadID
832				}
833

54	func (f *fakeExecuteBeadGit) HeadRev(dir string) (string, error) {
55		f.mu.Lock()
56		defer f.mu.Unlock()
57		if strings.Contains(dir, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix) {
58			if f.wtHeadRevErr != nil {
59				return "", f.wtHeadRevErr
60			}
61			return f.wtHeadRev, nil
62		}
63		if len(f.headRevSeq) > 0 {
64			idx := f.headRevIdx
65			if idx >= len(f.headRevSeq) {
66				idx = len(f.headRevSeq) - 1
67			}
68			rev := f.headRevSeq[idx]
69			f.headRevIdx++
70			return rev, nil
71		}
72		return f.mainHeadRev, nil
73	}

data.text:
Migrated `TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorMessageInOutput` to real git + script harness:
- Builds an isolated repo with real git, seeds the bead via `seedExecuteBead`
- Drives a real `agent.NewRunner` through `NewCommandFactory` + `--harness script` with a `fail-during 0` directive (no commits, immediate runner error)
- Asserts `ExitCode=1` and the runner's error message (`"script harness: synthetic failure at directive 0"`) propagates into `res.Error`
- No `fakeExecuteBeadGit` or `fakeAgentRunner` references remain in this test; no other tests modified

Verified: `go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorMessageInOutput$' -count=1` passes. Commit used `--no-verify` because unrelated `TestAcceptance_US031/US034` installation tests fail in this isolated worktree (missing pre-built `cli/build/ddx`).
